reason: Final Sign-Off is the release certification document and is fully traceable. §1 project identification (taskq, Python 3.14.6, 9 source modules, 496 statements, 175 tests, 11.03s) matches 05-verification/BASELINE.md §1-§2 and §3 verbatim. §2 pipeline completion table lists all gates 1-4 with scores that match .methodology/gate{1,2,3,4}_result.json. §3 Gate 4 composite 91.92 cited with explicit reference to .sessi-work/gate4_result.json::composite_score — above the 85 release threshold; per-dimension breakdown matches gate4_result.json::scores. §4 verification provenance covers both required artefacts (BASELINE.md + VERIFICATION_REPORT.md) and re-states all key metrics (FR/NFR scores, 175/175 PASS, 100% src coverage, bandit/gitleaks clean). §5 sign-off statement references SPEC.md v1.0.0 as source of truth and acknowledges known limitations in RELEASE_NOTES.md §8. Document is signed off by P6 Release Author on 2026-06-25.

gaps.message: Gate 4 composite cited as 91.92 (FINAL_SIGN_OFF.md:7, :43-44) references .sessi-work/gate4_result.json — but the committable .methodology/gate4_result.json (used by PhaseAuditor C10 + downstream CI) still holds 90.2222 (the pre-finalize agent-raw value; harness_cli.py:3201 patches the persistent copy on finalize-gate). Both numbers ≥85 release threshold (no blocker), but for sign-off integrity the persistent file should be re-finalized so the committable artefact matches the sign-off claim.

§3 mentions 'composite_score from .sessi-work/gate4_result.json' but Phase 7/Phase 8 entry gates read .methodology/quality_manifest.json::gate_results.gate4.overall_score (=90.22). Recommend the sign-off either (a) cite both numbers with explicit file pointers, or (b) re-run finalize-gate --gate 4 --phase 6 to bring the manifest in sync.
